meta_description: PatchPlane gives developers evidence-backed Patch Reports for AI-generated code changes before merge.
header_tagline: Evidence before you trust the patch
footer_description: Turn AI-generated patches into inspectable trust reports before they reach merge.
landing_title: Evidence before you trust the patch.
landing_lede: PatchPlane gives developers an evidence-backed Patch Report for every AI-generated change before it continues toward merge.
landing_stack_title: Meet developers where their AI patches already start.
landing_stack_intro: PatchPlane feels native beside the tools developers already trust, then adds the missing review lane before CI and merge.
landing_bento_review_title: One place for the request, run evidence, and reviewer decision.
landing_bento_review_body: Keep the change, agent context, identity, and live review state connected from the first request through the final approval.
landing_bento_review_step_1: Capture the request and repository owner.
landing_bento_review_step_2: Attach the isolated run and artifacts.
landing_bento_tools_title: Works beside the tools developers already use.
landing_bento_tools_body: PatchPlane fits beside source control, agents, auth, and deploy flows so review state follows the work instead of living in a separate checklist.
landing_bento_boundary_title: Keep untrusted code away from trusted automation.
landing_bento_boundary_body: PatchPlane keeps the agent path isolated, then promotes reviewed evidence into the trusted merge path.
landing_bento_boundary_1: No secrets in the first run
landing_bento_decision_title: Reviewers see the exact state they can approve.
landing_bento_decision_body: Every request carries sandbox checks, artifacts, diff context, and the reviewer gate in one traceable decision trail.
landing_trust_title: Built around the way developers already review code.
landing_trust_intro: PatchPlane proves trust with workflow artifacts, reviewer state, and a clear path from request to approval.
landing_trust_1_detail: Open-source surface for inspecting how patch review decisions are represented.
landing_trust_2_detail: Command-shaped intake that can sit beside existing issue and pull request habits.
landing_trust_3_detail: A review lane for untrusted generated changes before privileged automation runs.
landing_trust_4_detail: Evidence is useful only when it leads to an explicit reviewer decision.
landing_signal_intro_body: One clear loop from AI-generated patch to human decision.
landing_signal_1_detail: Keep the original prompt, repo, scope, policy, and owner attached before work begins.
landing_signal_2_detail: Execute generated changes in a separate environment before they reach trusted CI.
landing_signal_3_detail: Attach logs, artifacts, checks, risk notes, and approval state to the same patch.
landing_signal_4_detail: Keep the final merge path behind a readable human approval gate.
landing_signal_footer: AI patch → sandbox verification → Patch Report → human decision → GitHub result.
landing_workflow_title: AI agents create code faster than teams can verify it.
landing_workflow_intro: The hard part is not generating a diff. It is keeping the context, run evidence, and approval trail together long enough to trust what ships.
landing_workflow_1_summary: The original intent, repo scope, and owner often disappear once an agent starts changing files.
landing_workflow_2_summary: Terminal logs, generated artifacts, test output, and CI failures live in different places.
landing_workflow_3_summary: By the time a PR appears, teams still need to reconstruct what happened and whether it is safe.
landing_proof_title: A safer path from AI patch to merge.
landing_proof_intro: PatchPlane keeps the workflow familiar while adding the evidence developers need to approve generated changes confidently.
landing_proof_1_summary: Add a review lane before merge without replacing your existing pull request process.
landing_proof_2_summary: Run untrusted changes before they reach CI, secrets, shared caches, or privileged systems.
landing_proof_3_summary: See the original request, sandbox output, artifacts, checks, and review notes together.
landing_proof_4_summary: Know what shipped, why it passed, who approved it, and how to trace it back to the request.
landing_platform_title: Make the Patch Report the center of review.
landing_platform_intro: PatchPlane turns agent output into a readable trust report: what changed, what ran, what passed or failed, and what decision is still required.
landing_story_signal_shared_surface: One shared surface for agent runs and human reviewers
landing_story_signal_runtime_boundaries: Clear boundary before trusted CI, secrets, and merge paths
landing_hover_detail: A compact review cue gives developers enough context to trust the patch without pulling them into implementation detail.
landing_evidence_1_detail: The original request, repository, owner, and intended boundary stay attached to the patch.
landing_evidence_2_detail: Generated changes run away from trusted CI, secrets, and shared caches.
landing_evidence_3_detail: Changed files, logs, test output, and generated artifacts are grouped for review.
landing_evidence_4_detail: Reviewers can see what is waiting, what passed, and what still needs a human call.
landing_tab_request_title: Start with the intent, not just the diff.
landing_tab_run_title: Run generated changes before trusted automation sees them.
landing_tab_evidence_title: Keep logs, artifacts, checks, and risk notes together.
landing_tab_decision_title: Make approval an explicit part of the patch.
landing_decision_reviewers_detail: The agent can prepare the patch, but merge stays tied to a human decision trail.
landing_faq_1_answer: No. PatchPlane adds a review lane before or beside the existing GitHub pull request path.
landing_faq_2_question: How technical should the page get?
landing_faq_2_answer: Technical enough to feel credible to developers, but not enough to become architecture documentation. Product surfaces should do most of the explanation.
landing_faq_3_answer: One readable loop: request, isolated run, evidence, review, and decision. More integrations can come after that loop feels real.
landing_cta_title: Give every AI patch a safe path to merge.
landing_cta_body: Open the app to see the request-to-review flow, or inspect the open-source project on GitHub.
about_hero_title: Keep the workspace small until the execution loop is proven.
about_hero_lede: PatchPlane should earn its package boundaries by proving one real flow: request intake, runtime activity, review output, and a visible decision trail.
about_principles_title: Architecture notes belong here, not on the landing page.
about_principles_intro: These principles explain why the current workspace stays compact and where future extraction pressure should come from.
about_principle_1_detail: One TanStack Start app owns both the landing page and the browser product shell so the product language stays consistent while the loop is still forming.
about_principle_2_detail: `packages/backend` keeps Convex, runtime contracts, and sandbox boundaries together until the first execution path is real enough to justify extraction.
about_principle_3_detail: Runtime adapters, policy packages, and desktop-specific bridges should split only after request coordination, runtime normalization, and review orchestration are exercised end to end.
about_package_map_title: Each package has one clear job today.
about_package_map_intro: That keeps the codebase small enough to move quickly without hiding runtime decisions in too many layers.
about_package_1_role: Landing page, browser product shell, and shared visual language.
about_package_2_role: Convex backend root plus runtime, sandbox, and policy boundaries.
about_package_3_role: Shared types, schemas, workflow statuses, and capability language.
about_bootstrap_title: Local commands and near-term implementation work stay operational.
about_bootstrap_intro: This is the place for setup notes, current constraints, and the next slice of work that should turn the shell into a real control plane.
about_next_slice_body: Wire one request mutation, one fake run action, and one event feed from Convex before introducing more runtimes, shells, or packaging layers.
app_sign_in_prompt: Sign in to access authenticated Convex data.
app_auth_loading: Checking Convex authentication and exchanging the WorkOS token.
app_signed_out_workflows_intro: PatchPlane keeps workflow evidence, sandbox logs, and review state behind your workspace session.
app_workflow_prompt_placeholder: Describe the patch or review task PatchPlane should track.
app_workflow_start_signed_out: Sign in before starting a workflow.
app_workflow_start_no_org: Select an active WorkOS organization before starting a workflow.
app_workflow_start_success: Workflow started and recorded in Convex.
app_workflow_status_detail: Create a tracked review run with prompt, source, evidence, and trust state.
